The '''[[Wigan Warriors]]''' play [[Rugby League]] in [[Wigan]], [[England]]. Their 2017 season results in the [[Super League XXII]], [[2017 Challenge Cup]], and [[2017 World Club Series]] are shown below.

==World Club Challenge==
{{main|2017 World Club Series#World Club Challenge}}
As winners of the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]], Wigan Warriors qualified for the [[2017 World Club Series]]. The game saw them play [[Cronulla Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] whom they beat providing the [[Super League]]'s first ever win in the World Club Series era of the tournament.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/38971535|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 22-6 Cronulla Sharks|work=BBC Sport |date=19 February 2017}}</ref>

==Super League==
{{main|Super League XXII}}
Wigan's [[Super League]] form was relatively poor during the [[Super League XXII|2017 season]]. The year was the first time they had lost ten more games since [[Super League XIX|2009]], and the first time they had failed to qualify for the play-offs since [[Super League XVI|2006]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/competitions/super-league/results/2017-18 | title= Super League Results | publisher=Sky Sports | accessdate=28 January 2021}}</ref>

==Challenge Cup==
{{main|2017 Challenge Cup}}
As a "Super 8s" team of the [[Super League XXI|2016 Super League]], Wigan Warriors entered the [[2017 Challenge Cup]] in the sixth round where they beat [[2017 RFL Championship|Championship]]'s [[Swinton Lions]]. The quarter finals saw them beat local rivals [[Warrington Wolves]] by a single point.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40265452|title=Challenge Cup quarter-final: Warrington Wolves 26-27 Wigan Warriors|work=BBC Sport |date=17 June 2017}}</ref> In the semi-finals Warriors beat Salford Red Devils,<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40711179|title=Challenge Cup semi-final: Wigan Warriors 27-14 Salford Red Devils|work=BBC Sport |date=28 July 2017}}</ref> before finishing runners-up at [[Wembley Stadium|Wembley]] to [[Hull F.C.]] The final was their first final since 2013.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41012717|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 18-14 Wigan Warriors|work=BBC Sport |date=24 August 2017}}</ref>
